## 1. Role Justification

The UI Visual Artist owns the complete static UI visual asset library — icons, illustrations, button states, typography, store screenshots, and promotional art. This is a P1 hire because UI assets are required for Stage 0 (Art Direction) visual pillar definition and Stage 2 (Prototype) UI implementation. Without dedicated UI art, the prototype lacks visual fidelity and the game's first impression suffers.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 0, 1, 2, 5

# Position Specification Document

**Role:** UI Visual Artist (G21)
**Role Family:** Art / Creative
**Seniority:** Senior
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Art Division
**Reports To:** Art Director (Renaud Leclercq)
**Priority:** P1
**Compensation Band:** $100K–$135K base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G21-001
**Generated:** 2026-04-10T08:00:00Z
**Generated By:** CHRO Office (Automated Pipeline)

---

## 1. Role Justification

The UI Visual Artist owns the complete static UI visual asset library — icons, illustrations, button states, typography, store screenshots, and promotional art. This is a P1 hire because UI assets are required for Stage 0 (Art Direction) visual pillar definition and Stage 2 (Prototype) UI implementation. Without dedicated UI art, the prototype lacks visual fidelity and the game's first impression suffers.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 0, 1, 2, 5

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency                       | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method      |
| -------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| Game UI Art Production           | 25%    | ≥ 4/5             | Portfolio review       |
| Icon Set Design                  | 20%    | ≥ 4/5             | Icon design challenge  |
| Design-System Thinking           | 15%    | ≥ 4/5             | Case study             |
| Mobile UI Optimization           | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | Technical assessment   |
| Typography & Visual Hierarchy    | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Portfolio review       |
| Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Panel interview        |
| Production Velocity              | 5%     | ≥ 3/5             | Timed design challenge |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 5+ years game UI art experience
- Shipped 2+ mobile game titles with UI art responsibility
- Icon set production experience (100+ icons)
- Button state design expertise (multi-state systems)
- Typography hierarchy and mobile UI optimization knowledge
- Design-system thinking (reusable component libraries)

---

## 4. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                                      |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $100K–$135K (positioned at 75th percentile: $117K) |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 10% of base                                  |
| Equity            | 0.05%–0.10% (Senior tier)                          |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $15K (if leaving unvested equity)            |
| Relocation        | Up to $10K                                         |
| Conference Budget | $3K/year                                           |

---

## 5. Sourcing Channels (prioritized)

| Channel                        | Priority | Quality Weight |
| ------------------------------ | -------- | -------------- |
| King, Supercell, Rovio alumni  | 1        | 1.5×           |
| ArtStation UI/UX artists       | 2        | 1.3×           |
| GDC Art Track speakers         | 3        | 1.2×           |
| Referrals from Renaud Leclercq | 4        | 1.5×           |
| LinkedIn passive sourcing      | 5        | 1.0×           |

---

## 6. Assessment Battery

| Assessment                  | Format                     | Duration | Weight |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------- | -------- | ------ |
| Portfolio Review            | Automated rubric scoring   | Async    | 25%    |
| UI Art Challenge (Icon Set) | 48-hour async delivery     | 48h      | 25%    |
| Simulated Panel Interview   | AI panel, signal questions | 45 min   | 30%    |
| Behavioral / Culture Add    | STAR method                | 30 min   | 15%    |
| Design-System Case Study    | Component library design   | 24h      | 5%     |

---

## 7. Success Criteria

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                                                        |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Day 1**   | Figma/Photoshop access granted, art style guide reviewed, first icon task assigned              |
| **Week 1**  | Initial icon set draft (10 icons) reviewed by Art Director; feedback incorporated               |
| **Month 1** | Complete UI component library (buttons, panels, icons) approved; texture atlas pipeline defined |
| **Month 3** | All Stage 2 UI visual assets complete; production-ready for Stage 5 full content production     |

---
